summary: Linked the Kirby Yoshi-special object by shrinking `ftkirbyspecialyoshi.c` to the matching range `0x80109260..0x8010A930` and moving the remaining `0x8010A930..0x8010D740` code into a new still-NonMatching `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` unit. The PR also introduced `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.h` and retargeted includes from the old catch-all `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h` to the more accurate capture-yoshi header.
what_changed: `configure.py` added `Object(NonMatching, "melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c")` immediately after the now-matching Yoshi-special object.

`config/GALE01/splits.txt` split the old `ftkirbyspecialyoshi.c` range: `ftkirbyspecialyoshi.c` now ends at text `0x8010A930` and sdata2 `0x804D9560`, while new `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` covers text `0x8010A930..0x8010D740` and sdata2 `0x804D9560..0x804D95B0`.

Most of the deleted tail of `ftkirbyspecialyoshi.c` was reintroduced in new file `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` with 1499 added lines, preserving the nonmatching capture/egg and copied-special routines separately from the linked portion.

`ftkirbyspecialyoshi.c` removed now-unneeded effect, collision, Game & Watch pan, Peach Toad, and several static-data includes, added `ftKirby/ftkirbycaptureyoshi.h`, and replaced `ftKb_Init_804D9558` uses for z velocity with literal `0.0f` in the remaining matching code.

New `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.h` declares the moved public functions from `0x8010A930` onward, including `fn_8010A930`, `ftCo_CaptureKirbyYoshi_*`, `ftCo_KirbyYoshiEgg_*`, `ftKb_SpecialNMs_*`, `ftKb_SpecialNPe_*`, `ftKb_PeSpecialLw*`, and `ftKb_SpecialNGw_*` prototypes.
`ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h` removed a large block of unrelated Yoshi-capture and copied-special prototypes that now live in `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.h`.

`ftkirby.c`, `ftmotionstates.c`, `itkirbygamewatchchefpan.c`, and `itpeachtoad.c` now include `ftKirby/ftkirbycaptureyoshi.h` where they need moved declarations instead of relying on `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h` or local forward declarations.
`ftKb_Init.static.h` dropped static forward declarations for moved functions such as `fn_8010B2E8`, `fn_8010C344`, `fn_8010C44C`, and `fn_8010CFB0`.
smart_moves: Used an object-boundary split rather than trying to force the entire original `ftkirbyspecialyoshi.c` to match at once, allowing the proven prefix to link while isolating the still-problematic tail.
Kept the moved tail as `NonMatching` in build config, which preserves progress accounting without pretending the copied/capture section is solved.
Made the split explicit in `splits.txt`, including both text and sdata2 ranges, so linker layout remains controlled after introducing the new translation unit.
Created a dedicated `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.h` instead of continuing to expose Yoshi-capture symbols through `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h`, reducing misleading header coupling.

Replaced a static-data zero symbol reference in the matching `ftkirbyspecialyoshi.c` prefix with `0.0f`, consistent with the adjusted sdata2 split and helping the now-linked object avoid carrying data from the moved range.
Updated item and motion-state include sites to depend on the new header directly, which makes cross-system references to Kirby capture callbacks searchable and less accidental.
decomp_lessons: When a file is mostly ready but its tail still blocks matching, split at a real address boundary and mark only the solved object as `Matching`.
Always update both `configure.py` object status and `config/GALE01/splits.txt` ranges together when creating a new translation-unit boundary.
Do not leave moved prototypes in an unrelated legacy header just because that header previously happened to expose them; create a domain-specific header for the new unit.

A data-range split may require replacing or moving static-data references in the matching prefix; in this PR, `ftKb_Init_804D9558` references in the remaining Yoshi-special code became literal `0.0f`.

For large Kirby copied-special files, semantic boundaries may cut across names: this new `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` contains Yoshi capture/egg functions and also later copied-special code for Marth/Roy, Peach, and Game & Watch ranges, because the split follows linker addresses rather than perfect gameplay taxonomy.
naming_conventions: New file/header name uses lowercase project style: `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` and `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.h`.
Header guard for the new header is address-based: `GALE01_10A930`.
Function declarations keep address comments in the form `/* 10A930 */ void fn_8010A930(...)`.
Anonymous or not-yet-understood functions continue to use address names such as `fn_8010A930`, `fn_8010B124`, and `fn_8010CFB0`.
Known state callbacks use the fighter callback suffix convention: `_Anim`, `_IASA`, `_Phys`, `_Coll`.
Kirby copied-special prefixes remain specific to source ability, such as `ftKb_SpecialNYs_`, `ftKb_SpecialNMs_`, `ftKb_SpecialNPe_`, and `ftKb_SpecialNGw_`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Source split at `0x8010A930` made `ftkirbyspecialyoshi.c` independently matching while preserving the following nonmatching code in a separate object.
The new nonmatching file uses matching-control idioms already present in the diff, including `#pragma dont_inline on/reset`, local `inline` helpers, and many `PAD_STACK(...)` calls.
The remaining matching Yoshi-special code avoids the old `ftKb_Init_804D9558` sdata2 dependency by using `0.0f` for z velocity assignments.
Static forward declarations for moved functions were relocated near the moved implementations, reducing cross-object static declaration leakage.
review_feedback: No PR body, issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided slice, so there is no explicit human review feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that mark `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.c` as `Matching`.
Search for cleanup of `fn_8010A930` and other `fn_8010xxxx` names introduced in `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.h`.
Search for later refactors of `ftkirbycaptureyoshi.h` guard `GALE01_10A930` or prototype ownership.
Check whether other Kirby copied-special objects were linked using the same split-at-address tactic.
